title: net_pkt ownership semantics documented at each transfer point
description:
lichen_l2.c documents net_pkt ownership at every transition:

1. Line 197-203 (lichen_l2_send): 'Per Zephyr net_l2 contract: when L2 returns 0, it took ownership of the packet and must free it.'

2. Lines 497-503 (lichen_l2_input): 'On success: net_recv_data takes ownership. We MUST NOT access or unref pkt after this point. On failure: We retain ownership and must unref pkt.'

3. Line 511: 'pkt ownership transferred to network stack - do not access'

Zephyr's net_pkt ownership rules are subtle and error-prone (double-free or leak). These comments prevent bugs. If someone suggests removing them as 'obvious', push back: they're only obvious to someone who has memorized Zephyr's net_l2 contract.
close_reason: Positive review findings - worth defending, not fixing
